point_support.reason: The local context confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected people [doc1:chunk_00024].
The local context states that transmission occurs when infectious particles are breathed in or contact the eyes, nose, or mouth [doc1:chunk_00004].
The local context indicates that the risk of transmission is highest at close range but can occur over longer distances indoors [doc1:chunk_00024].
The local context mentions that COVID-19 can spread from touching contaminated surfaces and then touching the face [doc1:chunk_00004].
The local context confirms that people can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms and remain contagious for up to 20 days [doc1:chunk_00004].
External evidence supports that people can spread COVID-19 even if they are not showing symptoms.
External evidence indicates ongoing research into the most risky situations for COVID-19 spread.
External evidence supports that COVID-19 spreads through droplets from an infected person's mouth or nose.
External evidence confirms that COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms.
External evidence supports that COVID-19 spreads mainly through the air in tiny droplets.

The document states that COVID-19 spreads through the air in tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].
The document mentions that droplets can land on the face or be inhaled in areas with low airflow [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008].
The document confirms that COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007].
The document states that risk increases with close contact, poor airflow, and activities like talking or sharing a car [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00011].
The document notes that living with someone who has COVID-19 or spending time in crowded spaces increases the risk [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00010].
